corridors.name: Silk Board – Outer Ring Road

peak_windows.note: Silk Board junction is the worst bottleneck in Bengaluru — expect 40+ min for a 15 min stretch.
Evening outflow from Electronic City/Koramangala compounds here.

alternate_routes: HSR Layout inner roads bypass during peak, adds 2km but avoids junction stall.
Elevated corridor (if available) skips signal entirely.

corridors.name: Whitefield – Marathahalli Bridge

peak_windows.note: IT corridor inflow, Marathahalli bridge signal is the chokepoint.
Outflow from ITPL/tech parks stacks traffic back toward Kundalahalli.

alternate_routes: Old Airport Road via HAL adds distance but avoids bridge congestion.
ORR service road bypass for short stretches.

corridors.name: Hebbal Flyover Corridor

peak_windows.note: Airport-bound traffic + North Bengaluru IT inflow converge at the flyover.
Reverse flow, compounded by signal timing at the underpass.

alternate_routes: Bellary Road service lane during flyover congestion.
Outer Ring Road diversion via Nagawara for longer trips.

corridors.name: Electronic City – Hosur Road

peak_windows.note: Single-corridor bottleneck — Hosur Road has no real bypass, expect heavy stacking.
Worst evening corridor in the city per commuter reports — NICE Road tollway is the only real alternate.

alternate_routes: NICE Road (tollway) — faster but has toll cost, worth it during peak.
Konanakunte Cross route for local-only trips, not through-traffic.
